title: tests: improve build-with-network-test
body:
This test could be failed when we enable firewall.
To reduce the impact of firewall, we use "curl".

Signed-off-by: Toshiki Sonoda <sonoda.toshiki@fujitsu.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind failing-test 

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

This test will be failed in proxy environment.

comments.body:
> I don't think these cases are equivalent. I think the reason you're seeing a test failure is because you have a firewall that blocks outgoing ICMP, which is only incidentally related to proxies. I believe there are environments that block ICMP but not HTTP, and vice-versa, so this PR is just an unreliable heuristic.

Thank you for your review.

I understand that this root problem is not proxy but firewall.
I verified that ping default gateway is successful in my environment.

Therefore, I choose the method of ping default gateway.

> Therefore, I choose the method of ping default gateway.

CI tests are failed...
I choose curl again.

>CI tests are failed...
>I choose curl again.

Thank you for trying. Is there any chance you still have a window open showing the failure? Or at least remember the gist of it? I won't block, I'm just really curious.

/approve

@containers/buildah-maintainers PTAL

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan*, *sstosh* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Is there any chance you still have a window open showing the failure? Or at least remember the gist of it?

`ping -c 1 $gw (10.128.0.1)` is failed in Cirrus environment.
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4979492540645376?logs=integration_test#L1120-L1145

If we fix this issue, I think that we need to change the firewall rule about $gw (10.128.0.1) in Cirrus.
However, I don't know what the network configuration of Cirrus are.

<details>
<summary>Test log in Cirrus CI</summary>

Interesting! Very odd. Thank you for providing this info, I've saved it because I think it will help us in the future. And thank you again for all your contributions.
